game_name: Tiny Toon Adventures: Buster Busts Loose!

element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Progression from simple to complex enemy patterns
Available through exploration; reset after each level
Varies by level (e.g., feeding Dizzy Devil, defeating Duck Vader)
Chance to earn extra lives via character-specific minigames
Only accessible in Normal/Challenge difficulty; removed in easy mode

typical_relief_triggers: Reaching minigame wheel (safe interlude between levels)
typical_tension_triggers: Challenge mode constraint (1 health container only)
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Varied per level with between-level relief via minigame wheel; escalates in difficulty modes

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Exploration-based health container collection; levels are 'significantly smaller in easy mode'

difficulty_curve.early_game: Children mode offers significantly smaller levels with multiple health containers
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Normal mode introduces full-sized levels with standard health mechanics
difficulty_curve.late_game: Level 5 (Sky-jinx) serves as advanced content; Challenge mode reduces health to 1 container only
notable_spikes: Transition from Children to Normal difficulty (full level size increase)
Level 5 introduction (removed in easy mode entirely)
Challenge mode constraint (1 health container, 3 continues only)

notable_level_patterns.description: Each level has distinct objective (feeding Dizzy Devil in kitchen, defeating Duck Vader, etc.) rather than generic progression
Extra health containers available through exploration; encourages off-path investigation
Level 5 (Sky-jinx) only appears in Normal/Challenge modes; completely removed from Children difficulty

sources_used: Wikipedia - Tiny Toon Adventures: Buster Busts Loose! (Gameplay section)
The Cutting Room Floor - Tiny Toon Adventures: Buster Busts Loose! (Stage Select, Regional Differences)
